methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Velaura AI, a Series C frontier-chip company founded by Rajiv Khemani, has raised $600M+ to date. The valuation exercise benchmarks against a comp universe spanning 8 private and 3 public semiconductor and chip companies, including stage-adjacent peers like SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C).

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $600M total raised proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implied a $1.0B valuation. Peer-median benchmarking across the four identified comparables yielded a $1.9B valuation, with the peer set ranging from $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple was not feasible due to unavailable revenue data, a common constraint in private-company valuation.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $867M to $2.1B, with a median of $1.4B. The wide dispersion reflects the fundamental uncertainty in scaling privately-held semiconductor ventures and heterogeneity in stage and capital structures within the peer cohort. Key sensitivities remain Velaura's unrevealed revenue scale, capital efficiency relative to peers, and product-market traction in what remains a capital-intensive, competitive sector.
